title: myrtus australis
intro: Myrtus australis, also known as the Australian myrtle or southern myrtle, is a plant species native to eastern Australia. It is used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high content of essential oils, particularly eucalyptol and cineole. The medicinal properties of the plant have been linked to various health benefits, including relieving respiratory issues, soothing skin irritations, and supporting immune function. The herbal preparations of Myrtus australis often involve steam distillation to extract its essential oils, which can be used in aromatherapy, topical applications, or as a natural remedy in teas and infusions.
